compliance_patterns.description: Private enterprises follow internal procurement policies with vendor qualification requirements
Deliverables must align with corporate governance and board reporting requirements

evaluator_insights.insight: Private sector evaluators prioritize ROI, speed-to-value, and commercial pragmatism
evaluator_insights.implication: Proposals must quantify business impact and show clear return on investment
evaluator_insights.recommended_action: Include ROI projections, payback period estimates, and value realization timeline

evaluator_insights.insight: Corporate clients value industry peer benchmarks and competitive positioning
evaluator_insights.implication: Proposals should reference what competitors or industry peers have achieved
evaluator_insights.recommended_action: Include industry benchmarks and competitive analysis where relevant

evaluator_insights.insight: Private enterprises expect consultants to work at pace with lean governance
evaluator_insights.implication: Heavy-governance methodologies with excessive committee structures are off-putting
evaluator_insights.recommended_action: Propose lean governance with clear decision rights and rapid escalation

evaluator_insights.insight: C-suite sponsors evaluate based on strategic impact, not just technical completeness
evaluator_insights.implication: Executive summary and strategic framing carry disproportionate weight
evaluator_insights.recommended_action: Invest in a compelling executive summary that speaks to business transformation

forbidden_assumptions: Do NOT assume corporate clients are less rigorous than government — many have sophisticated procurement
Do NOT assume profit motive means the client will skip due diligence — corporate governance is strict
Do NOT assume the procurement contact is the decision-maker — map the full decision-making unit
